scored_prs.title: TYP: ``numpy.ma``: Annotate 27 functions related to masks and fill-values

analysis.pr_summary: This PR adds static type annotations to 27 previously untyped public functions within the `numpy.ma` module, improving typing coverage for masked arrays.
analysis.repo_summary: NumPy is a fundamental library for numerical computing in Python, providing support for large, multi-dimensional arrays and matrices, along with a large collection of high-level mathematical functions to operate on these arrays.
analysis.feasibility_notes: Reproducing involves running a type checker like `mypy` on the `numpy.ma` module before and after the changes to confirm that the new annotations are valid and contribute to improved type checking coverage.

scored_prs.title: TYP: ``ma.core`` annotate the remaining unannotated functions

analysis.pr_summary: This PR adds static type annotations to the remaining untyped functions in `numpy.ma.core` and fixes a bug where `ma.sort` incorrectly rejected `axis=None`.
analysis.repo_summary: NumPy is a fundamental library for numerical computing in Python, providing support for large, multi-dimensional arrays and matrices, along with a large collection of high-level mathematical functions to operate on these arrays.
analysis.feasibility_notes: Reproducing involves running a type checker on `numpy.ma.core` to verify the annotations and writing a test case for `ma.sort` with `axis=None` to confirm the bug fix.

scored_prs.title: TYP: fix np.number and np.*integer method declaration

analysis.pr_summary: This PR backports a fix for method declarations of `np.number` and `np.*integer` scalar types, continuing the series of static typing improvements from previous PRs.
analysis.repo_summary: NumPy is a fundamental library for numerical computing in Python, providing support for large, multi-dimensional arrays and matrices, along with a large collection of high-level mathematical functions to operate on these arrays.
analysis.feasibility_notes: Reproducing involves applying the backported changes to the target branch and verifying that the typing fixes for `np.number` and `np.*integer` are correctly applied and pass type checking.

analysis.pr_summary: This PR adds type stubs for the `numpy.matlib` module, improving static type checking capabilities.
analysis.repo_summary: NumPy is a fundamental package for numerical computing in Python, providing support for large, multi-dimensional arrays and matrices, along with a large collection of high-level mathematical functions to operate on these arrays.
analysis.feasibility_notes: Testing involves building NumPy and then running a static type checker (like MyPy) against code that uses `numpy.matlib` to ensure the stubs are correct and complete.

analysis.pr_summary: This PR fixes missing static typing re-exports in `_core.numeric` to reduce type checker warnings and align stubs with `numtype` verification.
analysis.repo_summary: NumPy is a fundamental library for numerical computing in Python, providing support for large, multi-dimensional arrays and matrices, along with a large collection of high-level mathematical functions to operate on these arrays.
analysis.feasibility_notes: Reproducing likely involves setting up a development environment with `mypy` and other type checkers, then running them against the NumPy codebase to observe the 'red squigglies'.

scored_prs.title: DOC Backport documentation search fix to 1.1.X

analysis.pr_summary: This PR backports a fix for the documentation search functionality to the 1.1.X branch.
analysis.repo_summary: Scikit-learn is a popular open-source Python library for machine learning, providing various classification, regression, clustering, and dimensionality reduction algorithms.
analysis.feasibility_notes: Testing involves building the documentation locally and verifying the search functionality works correctly.

analysis.pr_summary: This PR automatically updates various pre-commit hooks and code style tools like Ruff, Vulture, and clang-format to their latest versions.
analysis.repo_summary: pandas is a fast, powerful, flexible, and easy-to-use open-source data analysis and manipulation tool, built on the Python programming language.
analysis.feasibility_notes: Testing involves running `pre-commit run --all-files` and checking for any new formatting or linting issues introduced by the updates.

analysis.pr_summary: This PR removes a Windows-specific build shim to test its impact on the pandas build process.
analysis.repo_summary: Pandas is a fast, powerful, flexible and easy to use open source data analysis and manipulation tool, built on top of the Python programming language.
analysis.feasibility_notes: Testing this locally would involve setting up multiple build environments (especially Windows), compiling pandas, and running the full test suite to check for regressions or build failures.
